intent.operator_problem: EXEC-070 WS7A froze MetricType-to-authority routing rules and issued a Phase 1.1 waiver deferring public DTO field-shape changes. WS7B was separated into this child spec. Three defects remain in services/shift-intelligence/ that block WS9: (1) no routing helper exists — rules documented in EXEC-070 but not implemented; (2) getAlerts in alerts.ts assembles ShiftAlertDTO inline instead of calling mapShiftAlertRow, creating a divergent code path that Phase 1.2 would have to update in two places; (3) anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts asserts gamingDay/computedAt keys that do not exist on AnomalyAlertsResponseDTO — the live shape uses baselineGamingDay + baselineCoverage.
intent.pilot_fit: Internal correctness gate for services/shift-intelligence/ before WS9 can run items 4 and 12. Coherent single slice: all deliverables are service-layer only (no route, no UI, no SQL). The routing helper is the single shared primitive both mapper paths need before Phase 1.2 wraps public fields. The stale route-boundary test is a correctness defect, not a Phase 1.2 obligation.
intent.transitional_governance_note: The bare-number fields on AnomalyAlertDTO and ShiftAlertDTO are NOT bridge DTOs under the TRANSITIONAL-GOVERNANCE-CAVEAT. That caveat covers the visit-surface envelope bridge declared by PRD-072. Shift-intelligence bare fields are pre-migration state; Phase 1.2 wraps them directly without a bridge. This slice introduces no bridge DTO.
success_outcomes: resolveShiftMetricAuthority(metricType) implemented: drop_total → { type: 'estimated', source: 'table_session.drop' }, win_loss_cents → { type: 'estimated', source: 'table_session.inventory_win' }, cash_obs_total → { type: 'estimated', source: 'pit_cash_observation.extrapolated' }, hold_percent → null (bare ratio, never wrapped).
mapAnomalyAlertRow and mapShiftAlertRow both call resolveShiftMetricAuthority internally.
getAlerts in alerts.ts calls mapShiftAlertRow — inline DTO assembly block (lines 127–157) deleted.
anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts: gamingDay/computedAt keys removed from fixture and assertions; baselineGamingDay/baselineCoverage added matching AnomalyAlertsResponseDTO shape.
mappers.test.ts has one explicit assertion per MetricType routing rule including hold_percent returns null.
AnomalyAlertDTO and ShiftAlertDTO public numeric fields remain typed as number | null — no FinancialValue wrapper applied in this slice.
schemas.ts remains request-only per WS7A waiver — no outbound Zod added.
explicit_exclusions: AnomalyAlertDTO.observedValue, baselineMedian, baselineMad, thresholdValue wrapping in FinancialValue — Phase 1.2 per GATE-070.6
Outbound Zod schemas for AnomalyAlertDTO or ShiftAlertDTO — WS7A waiver; reopen requires lead-architect EXEC-SPEC amendment
UI component changes (components/shift-intelligence/, components/shift-dashboard/, components/admin-alerts/) — Phase 1.2 + 1.3
BaselineDTO numeric fields (medianValue, madValue, minValue, maxValue) — not in Phase 1.1 exception set per EXEC-070 Planning Lock Resolution
SQL migrations — no schema changes required
New bridge DTO declaration — this slice declares none; shift-intelligence bare fields are pre-migration state
WS9 verification matrix execution — stays under parent EXEC-070 as closing gate; runs after this slice closes
baseline.ts and baseline-computation.test.ts — no currency envelope treatment in Phase 1.1

adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Different bounded context, different pattern (mapper helper vs shape conversion); bundling defeats manageable-slice goal per EXEC-070 Amendment 1

adjacent_rejected.idea: Wrap observedValue et al. in FinancialValue now
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: GATE-070.6 deferred explicitly; fields cross live HTTP/UI boundaries — doing it now triggers full route+UI+component-test bundle that Phase 1.2 owns

adjacent_rejected.idea: Add outbound Zod schema for AnomalyAlertDTO
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: WS7A explicitly waived this; entangles internal routing with deferred public-shape decision — reopen trigger rule applies

adjacent_rejected.idea: Leave getAlerts inline assembly, fix in Phase 1.2
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: EXEC-070 explicitly flags as mandatory WS7B remediation; leaving creates two divergent update paths for Phase 1.2

adjacent_rejected.idea: Declare bridge DTO for bare-number shift-intelligence fields
adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Aligns with Transitional Governance Caveat pattern
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Caveat covers intentional dollar-float bridges (PRD-072). Shift-intelligence bare fields are deferred canonical state. Declaring a bridge creates governance debt without correctness benefit.

adjacent_rejected.idea: Fix stale route boundary test in WS9 instead of WS7B
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: WS9 item 12 cannot run at all if the fixture is wrong — test correction is a precondition for running WS9, not a phase-close nicety
Routing rules reference service-private metric identifiers; elevating to lib/ implies broader applicability that does not exist yet — keep service-private

entities.name: resolveShiftMetricAuthority
entities.description: Pure function mapping MetricType to { type: FinancialAuthority; source: string } | null. Returns null for hold_percent (bare ratio). Encodes the four WS7A-frozen routing rules.
entities.system_of_record: services/shift-intelligence/mappers.ts (or extracted to services/shift-intelligence/authority-routing.ts if both mappers.ts and alerts.ts need to import it)

entities.name: mapAnomalyAlertRow
entities.description: Existing mapper in mappers.ts. WS7B adds internal call to resolveShiftMetricAuthority. Public output shape (AnomalyAlertDTO) unchanged.

entities.name: mapShiftAlertRow
entities.description: Existing mapper in mappers.ts. WS7B adds internal call to resolveShiftMetricAuthority. Public output shape (ShiftAlertDTO) unchanged.

entities.name: getAlerts
entities.description: Existing function in alerts.ts. WS7B refactors inline DTO assembly (lines 127–157) to call mapShiftAlertRow from mappers.ts.

entities.name: AnomalyAlertsResponseDTO
entities.description: Existing response envelope. Fields: alerts: AnomalyAlertDTO[]; baselineGamingDay: string; baselineCoverage: BaselineCoverageDTO. This is the shape the stale test must assert.

entities.name: MetricType
entities.description: Existing union: 'drop_total' | 'hold_percent' | 'cash_obs_total' | 'win_loss_cents'. All four values must be explicitly covered by resolveShiftMetricAuthority.

relationships: resolveShiftMetricAuthority is called by mapAnomalyAlertRow and mapShiftAlertRow (single implementation path for both alert flows)
getAlerts calls mapShiftAlertRow — inline assembly deleted
anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts fixture must reflect AnomalyAlertsResponseDTO (baselineGamingDay + baselineCoverage), not the legacy shape (gamingDay + computedAt)
FinancialAuthority type is imported from types/financial.ts — not redefined in this service
hold_percent return value of null from resolveShiftMetricAuthority is a hard invariant — explicitly asserted in tests

capabilities.name: MetricType-to-authority routing helper
capabilities.description: resolveShiftMetricAuthority(metricType: MetricType): { type: FinancialAuthority; source: string } | null. Four branches: drop_total → { type: 'estimated', source: 'table_session.drop' }; win_loss_cents → { type: 'estimated', source: 'table_session.inventory_win' }; cash_obs_total → { type: 'estimated', source: 'pit_cash_observation.extrapolated' }; hold_percent → null. Exhaustive switch with compile-time exhaustiveness check.

capabilities.name: Unified mapper path for both alert flows
capabilities.description: mapAnomalyAlertRow and mapShiftAlertRow both call resolveShiftMetricAuthority. getAlerts in alerts.ts calls mapShiftAlertRow instead of inline assembly. Public DTO shape unchanged — routing result is used internally, not emitted on the DTO.

capabilities.name: Stale boundary test remediation
capabilities.description: anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts: ALERTS_FIXTURE corrected to { alerts, baselineGamingDay, baselineCoverage: { withBaseline, withoutBaseline } }. Route handler assertion updated: body.data.baselineGamingDay (string) + body.data.baselineCoverage (object with withBaseline/withoutBaseline). gamingDay and computedAt keys removed.

capabilities.name: Routing rule test coverage
capabilities.description: mappers.test.ts extended: one test case per MetricType routing rule, one explicit hold_percent → null assertion, one cash_obs_total static-threshold assertion (shouldEvaluate = false). alerts-mappers.test.ts extended: assertion that getAlerts produces ShiftAlertDTO via mapShiftAlertRow (mapper unification verified).

surfaces.name: shift-intelligence mapper
changes: Add internal routing call in mapAnomalyAlertRow
Add internal routing call in mapShiftAlertRow

surfaces.name: shift-intelligence alerts
changes: Refactor getAlerts to call mapShiftAlertRow; delete inline DTO assembly block (lines 127–157)

surfaces.name: mapper unit tests
changes: Add resolveShiftMetricAuthority test suite: one case per MetricType, hold_percent null assertion, cash_obs_total static invariant

surfaces.name: alert mapper tests
changes: Add test verifying getAlerts delegates DTO construction to mapShiftAlertRow

surfaces.name: route boundary test (remediation)
changes: Correct ALERTS_FIXTURE: remove gamingDay/computedAt, add baselineGamingDay/baselineCoverage

optional_surface.note: Extract resolveShiftMetricAuthority to a sibling file if both mappers.ts and alerts.ts need to import it. Decision at EXEC-SPEC time by lead-architect. If mappers.ts handles all routing use, keep it there.

excluded_surfaces.reason: Route handlers unchanged; public response shape unchanged in this slice
Remains request-only per WS7A waiver; no outbound Zod added in this slice
Public DTO shapes unchanged; no FinancialValue wrapper on public fields

business_rules.statement: resolveShiftMetricAuthority must cover all four MetricType values exhaustively. An unmatched MetricType is a compile-time error, not a runtime unknown.

hold_percent returns null from resolveShiftMetricAuthority. It is a bare ratio and MUST NOT be wrapped in FinancialValue at any layer in Phase 1.1 or Phase 1.2 (per EXEC-070 WS7 routing rules, frozen).

AnomalyAlertDTO and ShiftAlertDTO public numeric fields (observedValue, baselineMedian, baselineMad, thresholdValue) remain typed as number | null. No FinancialValue wrapper is applied in this slice. Phase 1.2 owns the wrapping under a separate EXEC-SPEC.

getAlerts in alerts.ts must call mapShiftAlertRow. Inline DTO construction is deleted. Both alert code paths (anomaly via mapAnomalyAlertRow, persistent via getAlerts → mapShiftAlertRow) use the same mapper.

anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts must assert baselineGamingDay (string) and baselineCoverage ({ withBaseline: number; withoutBaseline: number }) per AnomalyAlertsResponseDTO. Zero references to gamingDay (at response envelope level) or computedAt after remediation.
schemas.ts remains request-only. No outbound Zod schemas for AnomalyAlertDTO or ShiftAlertDTO are added. Reopening the WS7A waiver requires a lead-architect EXEC-SPEC amendment before execution.

cash_obs_total maps to { type: 'estimated', source: 'pit_cash_observation.extrapolated' } and the test suite preserves the static-threshold invariant: anomaly evaluation does not run for cash_obs_total.

If resolveShiftMetricAuthority is used by both mappers.ts and alerts.ts, extract it to a sibling file (authority-routing.ts) to avoid a circular import. If used only by mappers.ts, keep it there. Decision at EXEC-SPEC time.

triggers: RPC row arrives with metric_type string → resolveShiftMetricAuthority returns authority metadata → mapper uses it internally
Phase 1.2 wraps deferred public fields → calls resolveShiftMetricAuthority at the FinancialValue construction site
CI test run → mappers.test.ts exercises all four routing rules + hold_percent null invariant
CI test run → anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts exercises correct AnomalyAlertsResponseDTO shape
npm run test:slice:shift-intelligence → WS9 items 4 and 12 unblock

loop.action: rpc_get_anomaly_alerts / shift_alert table returns metric_type as string column
Maps MetricType to { type: FinancialAuthority; source: string } | null using frozen WS7A routing table. hold_percent → null.
Calls resolveShiftMetricAuthority internally. Public DTO fields (observedValue, baselineMedian, etc.) remain bare numbers — routing result is an internal invariant, not emitted on the DTO.
Calls mapShiftAlertRow from mappers.ts instead of inline assembly. ShiftAlertDTO emitted by the unified mapper path.
Passes AnomalyAlertsResponseDTO / ShiftAlertDTO[] through unchanged. Response shape is identical to pre-WS7B for consumers.
Asserts baselineGamingDay (string) + baselineCoverage ({ withBaseline, withoutBaseline }) on anomaly-alerts response. Asserts alerts[0].metricType is a valid MetricType value.
Explicitly assert all four MetricType routing rules: drop_total, win_loss_cents, cash_obs_total produce FinancialAuthority metadata; hold_percent returns null.

open_questions_allowed_at_scaffold.question: Should resolveShiftMetricAuthority live in mappers.ts or in a separate authority-routing.ts?
open_questions_allowed_at_scaffold.recommendation: Keep in mappers.ts if only used there. Extract to authority-routing.ts if alerts.ts needs to import it directly (to avoid circular import). Decision at EXEC-SPEC time.

open_questions_allowed_at_scaffold.question: Should resolveShiftMetricAuthority embed the authority result on the DTO in a non-public field for Phase 1.2 readiness, or remain a pure internal call whose result is discarded until Phase 1.2?
open_questions_allowed_at_scaffold.recommendation: Discard in Phase 1.1 — do not smuggle routing metadata into the DTO shape before Phase 1.2 formally wraps the fields. The routing call in Phase 1.1 is for testability and path-unification only. Phase 1.2 will call the same helper at the FinancialValue construction site.

open_questions_allowed_at_scaffold.question: Does anomaly-evaluation.test.ts need amendment as part of WS7B?
open_questions_allowed_at_scaffold.recommendation: Only if mapper refactoring creates a structural conflict. Current anomaly-evaluation.test.ts tests inline local logic functions, not imported mappers. Leave intact unless WS7B implementation reveals a conflict. If amendment is needed, scope it to the minimum change required.

open_questions_allowed_at_scaffold.question: Should the corrected anomaly-alerts-route-boundary.test.ts add a hold_percent metricType fixture to verify routing invariant at the boundary?
open_questions_allowed_at_scaffold.recommendation: Yes — add a second alert in the fixture with metricType: 'hold_percent' to ensure the route passthrough handles all MetricType values. This also exercises the null-routing branch without asserting FinancialValue (which does not exist on the DTO in Phase 1.1).

governance.expansion_trigger_rule: Amend this brief if any downstream artifact proposes: (a) wrapping AnomalyAlertDTO or ShiftAlertDTO public numeric fields in FinancialValue during this slice; (b) adding outbound Zod to schemas.ts without WS7A waiver reopen; (c) route handler changes in app/api/v1/shift-intelligence/; (d) UI changes in components/shift-intelligence/, components/shift-dashboard/, or components/admin-alerts/; (e) declaring a bridge DTO for shift-intelligence numeric fields; (f) parallel execution of mappers.ts and alerts.ts changes without serialization invariant being explicit; (g) widening scope to BaselineDTO numeric fields.

traceability.ws7a_decision_record: EXEC-070 § Planning Lock Resolution (routing rules frozen 2026-04-24)
shared_primitives: types/financial.ts (FinancialAuthority — used as return type of resolveShiftMetricAuthority)
lib/financial/schema.ts (financialAuthoritySchema — available if outbound Zod is reopened in Phase 1.2)
